breaking.text: Fed Beige Book flags weaker consumer spending and sticky energy/labor costs across multiple districts
Broadcom surges 5% after hours on blowout Q1: AI revenue up 106% to $8.4B, CEO touts $100B AI chip revenue pipeline
Broadcom reports fiscal Q1 earnings after the bell — analysts expect $2.03 EPS on $19.18B revenue
Coinbase surges 14.6% after Trump signals support for digital asset market bill
Trump backs crypto firms against banks on stablecoin yields, Coinbase surges 14%

summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,869.50 on Wednesday as markets staged a broad recovery from this week's Middle East-driven selloff, led by a 1.29% gain in the Nasdaq. Technology stocks powered the rally with Micron and AMD each jumping over 5%, while easing oil prices and stronger-than-expected ADP jobs data (63K vs 50K expected) bolstered sentiment. After the close, Broadcom delivered a blockbuster Q1 report with revenue of $19.3B and AI revenue surging 106%, sending shares up 5% in extended trading, while the Fed's Beige Book painted a mixed picture of slight-to-moderate growth but flagged weakening consumer spending and sticky inflation pressures.
